title: All Pairs Sum
description: Given an array of numbers and a target, return all unique index pairs [i, j] where i < j and nums[i] + nums[j] === target.
whatShouldHappen: Use an outer loop to pick the first element.
Use an inner loop starting one position ahead to pick the second element.
If the pair sums to the target, add [i, j] to the results.
Return all matching pairs.
patternExplanation: The inner loop starts at i+1 to guarantee i < j — this ensures every pair is checked exactly once without revisiting or counting a pair twice.

title: Multiplication Table
description: Given a number n, return a 2D array of size n×n where table[i][j] = (i+1) * (j+1).
whatShouldHappen: Create an empty outer array.
Outer loop runs n times, creating one row per iteration.
Inner loop runs n times, filling each column of the row.
Push the completed row into the outer array and return it.
patternExplanation: Nested loops where the outer index selects the row and the inner index selects the column — the standard pattern for building or traversing 2D grids.

title: Print Star Triangle
description: Given a number n, return an array of n strings where the i-th string (1-indexed) contains exactly i asterisks ("*"). E.g. n=3 → ["*", "**", "***"].
whatShouldHappen: Create an empty result array.
Outer loop runs from 1 to n (inclusive).
Inner loop runs i times, building a string of i asterisks.
Push each string into the result.
Return the result.
patternExplanation: The key insight: the inner loop bound is the outer loop variable itself (i). This makes the inner loop run more iterations each row, producing the triangle shape.

title: Matrix Diagonal Sum
description: Given a square 2D array (matrix), return the sum of both its main diagonal and anti-diagonal elements. If an element is on both diagonals (center of an odd-sized matrix), count it only once.
whatShouldHappen: Loop through row indices 0 to n-1.
Add the main diagonal element: matrix[i][i].
Add the anti-diagonal element: matrix[i][n-1-i].
If n is odd and i === Math.floor(n/2), both diagonals share the center — subtract it once to avoid double-counting.
Return the total sum.
patternExplanation: Both diagonals can be addressed in a single loop using index math: main diagonal is [i][i], anti-diagonal is [i][n-1-i]. The center-subtraction handles odd-sized matrix overlap.

title: Bubble Sort
description: Given an array of numbers, sort it in ascending order using the bubble sort algorithm. Return the sorted array (you may sort in place).
whatShouldHappen: Outer loop runs n-1 passes.
Inner loop compares adjacent elements: nums[j] and nums[j+1].
If nums[j] > nums[j+1], swap them.
After each pass, the largest unsorted element has bubbled to its correct position.
Return the sorted array.
patternExplanation: Bubble sort uses two loops: the outer counts passes and the inner does adjacent swaps. The inner bound shrinks by i each pass because the last i elements are already in their final position.
